auth.signUpSubtitle: After sign-up we will verify your email with a one-time code
auth.switchToSignIn: Already have an account → Sign In
auth.forgotPasswordSubtitle: We will send you a code to reset your password
auth.resetPasswordSubtitle: Enter the code from your email and choose a new password

errors.invalidCode: Invalid or expired code. Please try again.
errors.invalidCodeOrShortPassword: Invalid code or password too short (min. 8 characters).
errors.emailNotFound: Email not found or an error occurred. Please try again.
errors.accountExists: An account with this email already exists. Sign in instead.
errors.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
errors.wrongPassword: Incorrect password. Try again or reset your password.
errors.signInFailed: Sign in failed. Check your email and password.

program.title: 5/3/1 Program
program.description: A deterministic strength program by Jim Wendler. No guessing — your weights are calculated mathematically from your Training Max.
program.calibrationHint: Calibration determines your starting weights. We are not testing your max — go by feel.
program.pausedDescription: Training is paused. Your position and phase are preserved — you continue exactly where you left off.
program.resumeFailed: Failed to resume program, please try again
program.resetConfirmDescription: This will delete the entire 5/3/1 program including TM and history. You will need to go through calibration again.

phaseDescriptions.leader1: First high-volume block. Building strength and muscle mass.
phaseDescriptions.leader2: Second high-volume block. Continuing to build the foundation.
phaseDescriptions.anchor: Realization block. Lower volume, maximum intensity on AMRAP sets.
phaseDescriptions.seventh_week: TM Test or Deload between blocks.

calibration.title: 5/3/1 Calibration
calibration.subtitle: We will find your starting weights. This is not a max test — go by feel.
calibration.conservativeStart: Conservative start — progression will catch up
calibration.rpeHint: RPE 7-8 = you could do 2-3 more reps, but you don't need to.

tips.squat: Work up to a heavier squat set (approx. RPE 7-8). Leave 2-3 reps in the tank.
tips.bench: Work up to a heavier bench press set (approx. RPE 7-8). Leave 2-3 reps in the tank.
tips.deadlift: Work up to a heavier deadlift set (approx. RPE 7-8). Leave 2-3 reps in the tank.
tips.press: Work up to a heavier overhead press set (approx. RPE 7-8). Leave 2-3 reps in the tank.

calibration.templateSubtitle: Choose a supplemental template for your program

template.selectSubtitle: The template defines the supplemental work after main sets

workout.amrapQuestion: How many reps did you get? (AMRAP)
workout.finishConfirmDescription: We will move to the next day in the program.

history.editWarning: Editing will not affect training maxes or program progress.
history.deleteConfirmDescription: This workout will be permanently deleted from history. This action cannot be undone.

readiness.title: Readiness
readiness.noDataHint: Log how you feel today (and optionally HRV/sleep from your watch) to get a readiness recommendation for today's workout.
readiness.disclaimer: Guidance only, not medical advice. If you feel different from the numbers, trust yourself — the bar doesn't lie.

recommendations.push_pr: You're on today — feel free to push for a rep PR on the last (AMRAP) set.
recommendations.grind_min_reps: Hit the prescribed weights, minimum reps on AMRAP is fine — don't chase a PR. Do not reduce weights.
recommendations.consider_deload: Consider a lighter day or a deload. Decide for yourself — the data is only a guide.

settings.changesFromNext: Changes take effect from the next workout and will not affect automatic progression.
settings.missesOne: 1 missed target — next miss will automatically reduce TM by 10%
settings.missesMany: Repeated stagnation — consider resetting TM. Next miss will automatically reduce TM by 10%.
settings.pauseConfirm: Are you sure you want to pause the program? Position and phase will not change.
settings.leaderRecommendation: Forever recommendation: volume templates (BBB, BBS) in leader phases.
settings.anchorRecommendation: Forever recommendation: intensity templates (FSL, SSL) in anchor phase.
settings.tmSaveFailed: Failed to save Training Max, please try again
settings.pauseFailed: Failed to pause program, please try again

seventhWeek.title: 7th Week Protocol
seventhWeek.subtitle: Choose how you will spend this recovery week
seventhWeek.tipTmTest: TM Test verifies whether your Training Max is set correctly. You should be able to hit 3-5 strong reps.
seventhWeek.tipDeload: Deload is ideal if you feel fatigued. Light weights will aid recovery.
seventhWeek.tipDefault: TM Test is recommended after Leader phases. Deload is better if you need rest.

tm_test.name: TM Test
tm_test.description: Training Max validation. You should be able to hit 3-5 strong reps.

deload.name: Deload
deload.description: Active recovery. Light weights, maintaining movement patterns.

offline.conflictMessage: Workout{{detail}} was already completed on another device — offline changes to this workout were discarded.

readinessInput.subtitle: Readiness
readinessInput.title: How are you feeling today
readinessInput.disclaimer: Guidance only, not medical advice. Always listen to your body.

upcoming.tmMayChange: Weights are approximate — TM may increase after the cycle
upcoming.footer: Weights calculated from current TM · no progression prediction

stats.title: Statistics
stats.missingProfileDesc: We need your body weight and gender to calculate Wilks score and strength level.
stats.missingProgramDesc: Statistics will be calculated from your Training Maxes after completing calibration.
stats.amrapPerformanceDesc: Green = above target, red = below target
stats.footnoteWilks: Wilks and DOTS scores normalize strength by body weight and gender — enabling fair comparison between lifters of different weight classes.
stats.footnoteStrengthLevel: Strength Level shows where you stand relative to the population by bodyweight multiples. Elite = top 1% of trained population.
stats.footnoteE1rm: e1RM values are estimated from Training Maxes or AMRAP results.

profile.subtitle: Your profile helps personalize coaching. Limitations are always respected.
profile.title: Athlete profile
profile.exportDescription: Download all your training data — workouts, program, accessory logs.

autoreg.title: Autoregulated day
autoreg.subtitle: Enable if you consciously went lighter (fatigue, illness, bad day)
autoreg.notePlaceholder: e.g. Poor sleep, shoulder pain, work stress...
autoreg.noteHint: This note will not be used for progression calculations — for coach context only.
autoreg.warningText: Performance from this workout will not count toward TM progression. You will get a clean reading on the next cycle.

accessorySection.title: Accessory exercises
accessorySection.emptyNote: 5/3/1 is flexible — consistency in the main lifts is what matters.

meta.title: Power Diary — 5/3/1 training log without spreadsheets
meta.description: Training log for 5/3/1 Forever. Automatic weight calculations, plate calculator, rest timer, AMRAP and Training Max tracking, Wilks/DOTS statistics. No spreadsheets, no AI — just the program and math.

rows.bad: You forget how many plates to load
You don't know when to increase TM
You need a PC or phone with signal

hero.heading: Your 5/3/1 training.
hero.subheading: The app that calculates your weights, tracks cycle progress, and advances Training Maxes according to the methodology. You focus on lifting.

problem.heading: You know it from Google Sheets
problem.body: Blurry cells on mobile, manual percentage calculations, figuring out what to lift today. Power Diary does exactly what your spreadsheet does. Just for you, in one tap, without errors.

0.title: 5/3/1 Forever
0.desc: Leader, Anchor, and 7th week. The program calculates weights, percentages, and cycle progress for you. Nothing to track.

1.title: Automatic progression
1.desc: Log your AMRAP, the app calculates an e1RM estimate and advances the Training Max precisely per the methodology. No manual recalculation.

2.title: Plate calculator
2.desc: See exactly which plates to load for every set. No more mental math between sets.

3.title: Rest timer
3.desc: The rest countdown starts automatically after you check off a set. The screen stays on during your workout.

4.title: Stats that make sense
4.desc: Wilks, DOTS, e1RM over time, volume, accessory and body weight trends. See whether you are progressing.

5.title: Works in the gym
5.desc: Install to your home screen as an app. Offline mode lets you train even in a basement without signal.

0.title: Calibration
0.desc: Enter a few sets on the main lifts. The app calculates your Training Maxes from them.

1.title: Train
1.desc: Open today's workout, check off sets, log your AMRAP. Everything else is calculated.

2.title: Progress
2.desc: After each cycle, Training Maxes advance based on your performance. See your progress in the charts.

0.title: No AI, no guessing
0.desc: We rely on the proven math of 5/3/1, not a language model that could make up a weight. With strength training, it's about your health.

1.title: Your data is yours
1.desc: Download your full history in CSV or JSON at any time. No lock-in, no data selling.

2.title: Better than a spreadsheet
2.desc: No scrolling or typos in formulas. One tap logs a set, the app does the rest.

cta.heading: Run your next workout here
cta.body: Free, no install needed. Open in your browser and add to your home screen as an app.

footer.legal: Built on the 5/3/1 methodology by Jim Wendler. Not an official Jim Wendler LLC product.

cycleReview.title: Cycle {{cycle}} complete
cycleReview.tmAutoSet: TM for the next cycle was set automatically

bbb.name: Boring But Big
bbb.description: Classic hypertrophy template. 5 sets of 10 reps at 50-65% TM. High volume for muscle growth.

fsl.name: First Set Last
fsl.description: Strength and technique. 5 sets of 5 reps at the first working set weight. Less volume, more quality.

ssl.name: Second Set Last
ssl.description: Advanced FSL variant. 5 sets of 5 reps at the second set weight. Higher intensity.

bbs.name: Boring But Strong
bbs.description: Strength + volume. 10 sets of 5 reps at FSL weight. For advanced lifters.

charts.needAmrap2: Need at least 2 AMRAP results to display chart
charts.needWilksData: Need more data for Wilks trend
charts.needWeeks2: Need at least 2 weeks of data
charts.needBodyweight2: Need at least 2 body weight entries to display chart
charts.needWorkouts2: Need at least 2 workouts to display chart

finishDialog.title: Workout complete
finishDialog.subtitle: How did it go?
finishDialog.notesPlaceholder: How did it go? How did you feel? New PR?

plates.belowBar: {{weight}} kg is less than the bar ({{barWeight}} kg) — use a lighter bar or dumbbells.

accessoryPicker.title: Accessory exercises
accessoryPicker.descriptionPrefix: Select exercises for today's workout. Recommended categories:

upgrade.bannerSubtitle: Leader/Anchor cycles, more templates, 7th Week Protocol
upgrade.modalSubtitle: Your TM and history will be preserved. Choose a supplemental template:

accessoryTrends.noData: No records yet for this exercise.
